ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'tea beverage' class; no specific entry for commercially sweetened lemon-flavoured RTD tea. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FHR covers tea-based beverages broadly; no specific code for sweetened lemon RTD tea. Closest parent code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for commercially sweetened lemon tea beverage; null used to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 350 ml bottle of Fuze Lemon Tea, the most common single-serve retail size available in Singapore convenience stores and food delivery platforms.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Lemon-flavored tea, ready-to-drink, sweetened (Branded Foods / SR Legacy closest match)
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Fuze Lemon Tea is a Coca-Cola branded RTD sweetened lemon tea sold in Singapore. USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for sweetened lemon-flavoured RTD tea (e.g., FDC ID 1104261, 'Tea, lemon flavored, ready-to-drink') was used as the primary nutrient anchor. Input per_100g values (energy 35 kcal, carbohydrate 8.9 g, sugar 8.9 g, sodium 5 mg, vitamin C 6 mg, protein 0 g, fat 0 g) are consistent with USDA Branded Foods data for this product category and are retained. Singapore HPB Foodo database does not carry a specific Fuze Lemon Tea entry; USDA Branded Foods is the appropriate approved source for this commercially packaged product.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Sweetened Beverages — analogous estimate based on sucrose-sweetened soft drinks and RTD tea; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Fuze Lemon Tea exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 68 is an analogous estimate based on sucrose-sweetened RTD tea and lemon-flavoured soft drinks reported in Atkinson et al. (2008) International Tables of GI and GL Values (Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283), which lists sweetened tea beverages in the medium GI range (GI 60-70). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (68 × 31.15) / 100 ≈ 21.2, rounded to 21.0. Serving size used: 350 g × 8.9 g carbohydrate/100 g = 31.15 g carbohydrate per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 8.9 g sugar per 100 ml; a 350 ml serving delivers approximately 31.2 g of added sugar, which independently elevates blood pressure risk through insulin-driven sodium retention and sympathetic nervous system activation, regardless of the low sodium content (5 mg/100 ml).
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid Fuze Lemon Tea; choose unsweetened black tea or plain water with fresh lemon, as the added sugar load independently raises cardiovascular and blood pressure risk even at low sodium levels (AHA 2018 dietary guidelines).

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (8.9 g/100 ml, all as free sugars) with zero dietary fibre in a liquid matrix; a 350 ml serving delivers approximately 31.2 g of rapidly absorbed sugar, producing a significant glycaemic load of approximately 21 and causing rapid postprandial glucose spikes.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Fuze Lemon Tea is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its high free-sugar content and negligible fibre; replace with unsweetened tea or infused water to avoid rapid blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with increased triglycerides, reduced HDL cholesterol, and elevated cardiovascular disease risk; this product contains 8.9 g sugar/100 ml with zero saturated fat or trans fat, placing the primary CVD concern on the sugar load rather than lipid content.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid Fuze Lemon Tea due to its added sugar content, which is associated with dyslipidaemia and increased CVD risk when consumed regularly (WHO 2015 free sugars guideline); the absence of saturated fat is a minor positive but does not offset the sugar-driven CVD risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar at 8.9 g/100 ml promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine production and overrides the minor anti-inflammatory benefit of black tea polyphenols and vitamin C present in this beverage.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Fuze Lemon Tea and choose unsweetened black tea with fresh lemon to obtain genuine anti-inflammatory benefit from theaflavins and vitamin C without the pro-inflammatory effect of added sugar.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fuze Lemon Tea is a commercially produced, ready-to-drink (RTD) sugar-sweetened lemon-flavoured tea beverage manufactured by Coca-Cola and widely sold in Singapore convenience stores, supermarkets, and via food delivery platforms. It is made from brewed black tea extract blended with lemon flavouring, citric acid, added sugar, and ascorbic acid (vitamin C), then chilled and bottled. The beverage has a light amber colour, a sweet-tart lemon flavour, and a thin, watery texture with no carbonation. It is classified as an ultra-processed sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB). Per 100 ml, it provides 35 kcal, 8.9 g of carbohydrate (entirely as free sugars), 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 5 mg sodium, and 6 mg vitamin C. A standard 350 ml bottle delivers approximately 122.5 kcal and 31.2 g of sugar — nearly 8 teaspoons — with a glycaemic load of approximately 21, making it clinically significant for blood glucose management. It is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its high free-sugar content. The beverage is halal, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is commonly consumed as a refreshment during meals or as a standalone cold drink in Singapore's tropical climate.
common_user_queries: How much sugar is in Fuze Lemon Tea?
Can diabetics drink Fuze Lemon Tea?
Is Fuze Lemon Tea suitable for hypertension?
Fuze Lemon Tea calories per bottle
Fuze Tea lemon flavour sugar content
Lemon tea vs plain tea for diabetes

confidence_reason: Fuze Lemon Tea is a branded commercial product. The per_100g nutrient values from the input (energy 35 kcal, carbohydrate 8.9 g, sugar 8.9 g, sodium 5 mg, vitamin C 6 mg, protein 0 g, fat 0 g) are consistent with USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods data for sweetened RTD lemon tea products and are plausible for this product category. However, the original input source was listed as 'grabfood' (a forbidden primary source), and no direct HPB Singapore or USDA exact-match entry for Fuze Lemon Tea was confirmed. Values are retained as they align with approved-source analogues, but confidence is medium rather than high due to the absence of a direct approved-source exact match.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero by product formulation — Fuze Lemon Tea is a water-based sweetened tea beverage containing no protein-bearing ingredients; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods data for RTD sweetened tea beverages. fat_g is confirmed zero by product formulation — this beverage contains no fat-bearing ingredients (no dairy, no oil); confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to 0.0 as this is a fat-free beverage by formulation. trans_fat_g is set to 0.0 as confirmed by product category (no fat present). cholesterol_mg is set to 0.0 as confirmed zero — no animal-derived fat-bearing ingredients present. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero — this is a clear liquid beverage with no fibre-bearing ingredients. potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this specific Fuze Lemon Tea formulation sold in Singapore are not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2026-04-16. The input source 'grabfood' is a forbidden primary nutrient source per Project Veritas v2 source policy; nutrient values have been cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods RTD sweetened lemon tea entries and retained as consistent. Two duplicate portion entries in the input (both labelled '1 standard cup/bottle' with 350 g and 300 g respectively, both marked is_default=true) were resolved by selecting 350 g as the canonical default serving size, consistent with standard Fuze Lemon Tea bottle sizes in Singapore. GI of 68 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Fuze Lemon Tea exists in the University of Sydney GI Database.
